wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering new CSP positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Daily chart, 20-period 2σ Bollinger Bands. Lower BB estimated ~$8.50–9.50 given high volatility. Wait for price to tag or breach lower band before selling CSP. Current price $10.59 is mid-band.

csp.annualisedYield: ~45–80% annualised on capital at risk (small cap, high IV benefit)
csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured OR close 5 days before earnings. Do not hold through earnings.
csp.note: Small cap defense/railway tech. IV likely ~80–120%. Very low capital at risk per contract ($800–$950). Size to 3–5 contracts given low individual contract cost. If assigned, cost basis $8–9.50 is ~10–25% below current price.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium. Re-sell monthly to accumulate premium. Close before earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned, sell CC slightly above cost basis for additional yield. High IV makes CC premium attractive relative to stock price.

exitRules.entryTrigger: Stock at $9.06 — below analysis price ($10.59), now 14% cheaper entry. Wait for post-May-earnings dip to $8.00–9.00 (lower Bollinger Band ~$8.50–9.50) before adding LEAPS.
exitRules.profitExit: Close LEAPS at 50–60% gain on premium. First target: stock at $14–15 (+55–65%) for ~$3–4 intrinsic. Let half ride to $20 analyst target for $9+ intrinsic.
exitRules.timeStop: Review LEAPS position if stock has not cleared $12 by November 2026. Roll out to May 2028 if thesis remains intact but stock is slow to move.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40% loss on LEAPS premium. Do not let a LEAPS decay to zero — roll or close if stock breaks below $7.50.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close or roll before ~August 10 (5 days before August earnings). IV at 85% means IV crush is severe post-earnings. Roll to new LEAPS post-earnings at lower IV.

options.bestFor: Avoid — only 5 days at close before August earnings. Almost no time value remains at the mandatory exit point.
Borderline amber — 40 days at close is workable for a short-term trade. But ONDS needs multiple quarters to realize its defense contract thesis. Too short for the LEAPS story.
Better — covers Q3 earnings and gives until late 2026 for the autonomous systems thesis. Still short relative to the 360-day LEAPS thesis. Consider only if LEAPS are illiquid.

Recommended — matches the original 360-day LEAPS thesis. 278 days of time value at close. Only vehicle that gives Ondas Holdings enough time to execute its autonomous railway and drone defense contracts.

recommendation.title: 360-Day LEAPS — ATM Call
recommendation.entryNote: High IV (~80–100%). In high-IV environments ATM delivers better returns than deep OTM for a 90% expected move. Do NOT buy the $20 call despite open interest — you'd be closing it barely ATM at your exit target.

companyOverview: Transformed from small drone startup to US defense prime contractor via 5 acquisitions. Operates Ondas Autonomous Systems (AI drones for ISR/border security) and Mistral Inc. (acquired April 24 2026 — prime contractor with Army/SOCOM IDIQ access). Palantir AI partnership integrated across all platforms.
catalysts: Mistral acquisition (Apr 24) — prime contractor status, Army/SOCOM IDIQ access, +$264M backlog
Palantir AI partnership — credibility and government customer access
Israel $1.7B border security program — $10M entry order, gateway to hundreds of millions more
$1.5B cash — 3+ years runway, no forced dilution needed
risks: $375M 2026 guidance requires 640% growth — very aggressive ramp (Q2–Q4 needs avg $112M/quarter)
Not yet profitable — targeting EBITDA positive H2 2026
P/S 13.4x on FORWARD guidance; 99x on trailing revenue
121.58M warrants at $28 strike — dilutive if stock exceeds $28 (above all analyst targets)
Institutional buyer from Jan offering underwater at $16.45 — potential selling pressure on recovery

dilution.summary: Most dilution already happened. Monitor warrants at $28.

items.label: Jan 2026 $1B offering (done)
items.detail: 60.79M new shares issued at $16.45 (+14% dilution). Priced at premium — institutional buyer currently underwater at $10.59.

items.label: $28 warrants (121.58M shares)
items.detail: Only dilutive above $28. Stock needs 164% gain from current price. Above all analyst targets. Low near-term risk.

items.label: Future acquisitions
items.detail: $1.5B cash means less need for stock-based acquisitions. Lower risk than feared.
